exemplar.title: To what extent do the attachment styles developed in early life affect emotional expressiveness in adult romantic relationships?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent do the attachment styles developed in early life affect emotional expressiveness in adult romantic relationships?

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: The topic and research question are communicated clearly and reinforced throughout the introduction, chapter headings, and conclusion.
Definitions of background concepts (attachment theory, emotional expressiveness) are precise and consistently linked to the research focus.
The research question is sharply formulated, focused, and effectively connected to the discussion across most sections.
weaknesses: Chapter 3 occasionally drifts into standalone discussions without explicit linkage back to the core research question.
The absence of a dedicated methodology section limits transparency about search strategies, inclusion criteria, and selection rationale.
Minor wording issues (e.g., use of second-person pronouns) slightly blur academic precision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation communicates its topic and purpose accurately and maintains clear focus on the research question, meeting the descriptors for the top band (5â6). Only minor lapses in explicit linkage and methodological transparency prevent an exemplar edge, but overall the studentâs work fully satisfies the expectations for a 6.

strengths: The student demonstrates good subject knowledge, accurately summarising foundational and contemporary studies in attachment theory.
Key constructs are described with clarity and applied correctly to the empirical findings.
Terminology such as âsecure attachmentâ, âemotional expressivenessâ, and âsocial desirability biasâ is used largely accurately and consistently.
weaknesses: Source application is often descriptive rather than synthetic, with limited interpretive commentary.
Inclusion of popular-media material (e.g., Cosmopolitan, TikTok statistics) dilutes academic rigor.
Some terms (e.g., âemotional expressivenessâ, biological factors) are imprecisely operationalised, and casual phrasing reduces scholarly tone.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Knowledge and understanding are solid and falls squarely in the competent (3â4) band. The work shows clear grasp of concepts and terminology, but the reliance on less scholarly sources and limited synthesis preclude higher marks in this criterion.

strengths: The research base is broad and mostly relevant, drawing on seminal longitudinal studies and naturalistic observations.
Analysis is clear, with reasoned connections between study findings and attachment-based predictions.
The argument is structured and acknowledges methodological caveats (self-report bias, ecological validity) and study limitations.
weaknesses: Depth of analysis is limitedâthere is little comparative assessment of effect sizes, sample characteristics, or study design.
Counter-evidence is described but not rigorously weighed to strengthen the evaluative stance.
Discussion of theoretical implications and integration back to the âto what extentâ question is uneven and sometimes superficial.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates good critical thinking consistent with the 7â9 band: research is mostly relevant, analysis is clear with supported conclusions, and the argument shows partial critical evaluation. The work falls short of the highest band only due to moderate depth and uneven evaluation.

strengths: The report includes all major structural elements (title page, table of contents, chapter organization, conclusion, bibliography).
Required front-matter (word count, page numbering) is present and complete.
weaknesses: Heading hierarchy is inconsistent, with mixed formatting and repeated numbering.
Referencing style is uneven, with inline URLs, incomplete author details, and duplicate entries.
Minor formatting errors (broken links, misaligned subheadings) and shifts in tone (second-person pronouns) disrupt professional presentation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets the basic structural and formatting requirements but contains several weaknesses in heading consistency, referencing conventions, and formal tone. This performance aligns with the 1â2 band, meriting a score of 2.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Strengths**
â¢ Terminology such as âsecure attachmentâ, âemotional expressivenessâ, âdismissive-avoidantâ, âecological validityâ, and âsocial desirability biasâ is used largely correctly and aids clarity.
â¢ Definitions of attachment styles are provided early, and subsequent usage remains consistent.

**Weaknesses**
â¢ Some terms are imprecisely operationalised (e.g., âemotional expressivenessâ remains broad; biological factors conflated with oxytocin without clear explanation).
â¢ Minor lapses (second-person âyouâ, casual phrases) reduce academic tone.

The mostly accurate yet occasionally uneven terminology aligns with the middle band (3-4).

**Strengths**
â¢ Required front-matter elements (word count, candidate code) are present; basic layout (paragraphing, page breaks) is acceptable.
â¢ Pages are numbered and the text remains within word-limit.

**Weaknesses**
â¢ Referencing style is inconsistent (mixture of superscripts, inline URLs, and incomplete author details) and duplicates occur in the bibliography.
â¢ Minor punctuation and formatting errors (e.g., broken links, mismatched DOI formatting) detract from professional appearance.

Overall, some layout conventions are met, but several are incorrectly applied, fitting the 1-2 band.

**Strengths**
â¢ The student summarises numerous foundational and contemporary studies, demonstrating solid factual grasp of attachment theory and emotional expressiveness.
â¢ Key constructs (secure, anxious-preoccupied, dismissive-avoidant, disorganised) are described accurately and applied to empirical findings.

**Weaknesses**
â¢ Application of sources is often descriptive rather than interpretive; quotations or paraphrases are presented with limited synthesis.
â¢ Inclusion of popular-media or less scholarly material (Cosmopolitan article, TikTok statistics) dilutes academic rigor.

Overall knowledge is good and sources are mostly relevant, situating the work in the 3-4 band at a competent level.

**Strengths**
â¢ The essay draws on a reasonably wide range of peer-reviewed journal articles and classic attachment studies, indicating some effort at informed source selection.
â¢ Basic research procedures (literature review, thematic chapter structure) are evident.

**Weaknesses**
â¢ There is no explicit methodology section outlining search strategies, inclusion criteria, or the rationale for selecting key studies; the reader cannot gauge the systematic nature of the research process.
â¢ Some cited works (e.g., DeWalt et al., 2011) appear tangential to the research focus, suggesting incomplete screening for relevance.
â¢ Little evidence of methodological reflection (e.g., consideration of study designs, sampling, ethics).

Given a mostly adequate but not comprehensive or transparent methodology, performance aligns with the 3-4 band.

**Strengths**
â¢ The studentâs title and opening paragraphs state the topic and research question explicitly, and the subsequent chapters, headings and signposting reiterate the central focus on how early-life attachment styles influence emotional expressiveness in adult romantic relationships.
â¢ Background concepts (attachment theory, emotional expressiveness) are defined in accessible language and linked to the stated purpose, giving readers a clear sense of direction.

**Weaknesses**
â¢ Occasional drifts occur in Chapter 3 (environmental/biological/cultural factors) where links back to the core research question are implied rather than explicitly reinforced.
â¢ Some minor wording issues (e.g., second-person âyouâ) slightly blur precision.

Overall, topic communication is accurate and generally effective, with a clear and appropriate purpose. Minor lapses prevent a full exemplar performance, placing the work solidly in the 5-6 band.

**Strengths**
â¢ The majority of cited studies (Feeney & Noller; Simpson et al.; Hazan & Shaver; Collins & Feeney) are seminal and clearly relevant to the question.
â¢ Evidence spans classic laboratory work, longitudinal designs and naturalistic observations, giving reasonable breadth.

**Weaknesses**
â¢ A few sources are of questionable pertinence (DeWalt et al. on health-literacy toolkit) or popular media, indicating gaps in screening.
â¢ No discussion of database scope or exclusion of non-relevant literature, limiting confidence in comprehensive coverage.

The research base is good and mostly relevant, matching the 4-6 band mid-point.

**Strengths**
â¢ The research question is sharply formulated (âTo what extent do the attachment styles developed in early life affect emotional expressiveness in adult romantic relationships?â) and is consistently referenced in the introduction, chapter titles and conclusion.
â¢ Most sections (Chs 1â2, Discussion, Conclusion) return to this question, maintaining a coherent argumentative thread.

**Weaknesses**
â¢ The integration in Chapter 3 is weaker; subsections on environment, biology and culture sometimes read as stand-alone mini-essays rather than tightly focused explorations of the research question.
â¢ Occasional summary statements lack explicit linkage to the guiding âto what extentâ element.

The question is clear and focused, and is usuallyâbut not alwaysâkept at the centre of the discussion; this merits the upper band (5-6) though not the highest point.

**Strengths**
â¢ The essay follows the required EE elements: title page, abstract replacement (not needed for current guide), table of contents, introduction, main body, conclusion and bibliography.

**Weaknesses**
â¢ Inconsistent heading hierarchy (mix of bold, numbered and unnumbered subsections) disrupts flow; some subsection numbering repeats.
â¢ Placing of chapter numbers within the table of contents is misaligned; occasional page references are missing or inaccurate.

Thus, structure is generally appropriate but contains several weaknesses, aligning with the 1-2 band (score 2).

**Strengths**
â¢ The discussion section groups findings, notes complexities (environmental, biological, cultural modifiers) and recognises study limitations, showing emergent evaluation.
â¢ The conclusion revisits limitations and recommends future research, demonstrating some critical stance.

**Weaknesses**
â¢ Evaluation remains surface-level; conflicting evidence is acknowledged but not deeply dissected, and theoretical implications are sketched rather than argued.
â¢ Integration of critique back into the overarching âextentâ question is uneven.

The argument is reasoned with partial critical evaluation, corresponding to the 4-6 band at a moderate level.

**Strengths**
â¢ The student connects study findings to attachment-based predictions and contrasts secure versus insecure patterns, producing clear (if brief) analytical statements.
â¢ Some acknowledgement of methodological caveats (self-report bias, ecological validity) appears in the conclusion.

**Weaknesses**
â¢ Analysis is mainly summative; little depth is given to comparing sample characteristics, measuring tools or effect sizes across studies.
â¢ Counter-evidence is presented but not rigorously weighed; conclusions about âto a certain extentâ remain generic.

Consequently, analysis is clear but only partially supported, placing it in the 4-6 band toward the lower end.

annotations.comment: The table of contents is provided, enhancing navigation and satisfying structural conventions.
The research question is stated clearly and prominently, providing a focused foundation for the investigation.
The title effectively communicates the investigationâs topic and establishes immediate context.
Word count is clearly presented on the title page, meeting the extended essay formatting requirement.
Summary of Feeney and Nollerâs methodology lacks critique of sample size, measurement validity, and bias; integrate methodological evaluation to strengthen research quality.
Footnote is missing page numbers and publisher info; standardize citation style consistently throughout the text.
Inconsistent alignment and missing indentation of subheadings reduce clarity; refine numbering and indent levels to improve structure.
The introduction effectively defines attachment theory with an accurate source reference, demonstrating solid subject knowledge.
The description of attachment styles is clear but lacks explicit linkage to the central research question; integrate reference back to adult romantic relationships to maintain focus.
Incorporating socialâmedia and popularâmedia statistics dilutes academic rigor; prioritize peerâreviewed empirical evidence to bolster credibility and relevance.
The shift from thirdâperson to secondâperson pronouns disrupts academic tone; revise language to maintain consistent formality.
A dedicated methodology section is missing; include search strategies, inclusion criteria, and selection rationale to enhance transparency and replicability.
Hazan and Shaverâs newspaper survey carries selfâselection bias and limited generalizability; discuss these sampling limitations to contextualize findings.
The narrative of Bowlby and Ainsworth studies is descriptive without critical analysis; explicitly relate their findings to emotional expressiveness for deeper insight.
The overview of dismissiveâavoidant and disorganized styles remains descriptive; compare effect sizes or sample characteristics across studies to strengthen analytical depth.
Citation for Feeney and Noller (1990) is incomplete; include full publication details and DOI to ensure accurate reference formatting.
The placeholder âfirst lastâ indicates missing author data; update with correct author names and publication details for accuracy.
The description of Simpson et al. (1990) ends midâsentence, disrupting readability; ensure manuscript completeness and coherence.
Footnote marker â19â appears without inâtext superscript; correct numbering to link footnote content with the text.
Misalignment between the DeWalt et al. reference and the described acetaminophen study indicates a source mismatch; verify and correct empirical evidence.
Fraley and Shaverâs covert observation raises ethical issues regarding informed consent; address participant ethics to strengthen critical evaluation.
Chen et al.âs cultural study lacks an inâtext superscript citation; include proper referencing to maintain scholarly conventions.
The discussion effectively summarizes studies but lacks deeper evaluation of conflicting evidence and theoretical implications; enhance critical synthesis here.
The discussion does not fully revisit the âto what extentâ research question; explicitly connect findings back to this guiding query to reinforce focus.
Synthesis of environmental, biological, and cultural factors is insightful but lacks integration into a cohesive argument; clarify how each modifier interacts with attachment theory.
Bibliography contains duplicate entries for the same study; remove redundancies and consolidate references for concise formatting.
Bibliography entries are not alphabetically ordered and vary in style; standardize ordering and formatting to enhance professional presentation.
